title: Persona
description: Top-level persona YAML. One persona = one shipping hardware configuration.

display_name.description: Human-readable name shown in the coverage-grid output.

dmi.description: DMI fields mapped 1:1 to `/sys/class/dmi/id/<field>` — QEMU's `-smbios` flags inject these at boot time.

id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier. Must match the YAML filename (without the `.yaml` extension).

kernel.description: Kernel lockdown mode for the booted kernel.

quirks.description: Advisory list of vendor-specific quirks the harness can't simulate. Surfaced to the scenario runner so test reports can annotate "this would work here but not on real hardware because X".

scenarios.description: Per-scenario run/skip overrides. Opt-out is rare — prefer fixing the scenario over skipping it per-persona.

schema_version.description: Pins the parser to an exact schema version. Mismatched parsers refuse to load the file. Bumped on breaking changes; additive fields don't bump it.

source.description: Provenance record. Every persona must cite where its DMI + firmware values came from. See `Source` docs for accepted kinds.

tpm.description: TPM version to emulate via swtpm (or skip entirely).

vendor.description: SMBIOS `sys_vendor` value verbatim as the vendor ships it. Preserve case (e.g. "LENOVO", "Dell Inc.", "Framework").

year.description: Year the SKU first shipped. Optional but recommended — helps contributors understand firmware-era context.

Dmi.description: DMI fields mapped to `/sys/class/dmi/id/<field>`. Everything QEMU's `-smbios type=0/1/2/3/...` injectors can populate.

chassis_type.description: SMBIOS chassis type code (10 = notebook, 3 = desktop, 17 = main server chassis, etc.). See SMBIOS spec §7.4.1.

product_name.description: Mapped to `/sys/class/dmi/id/product_name`. SKU code or friendly name per vendor convention (Lenovo puts the friendly name in `product_version`; Dell/HP put it here).

Kernel.description: Kernel config for the booted kernel. `Default` leaves the initramfs kernel's lockdown mode unchanged.

lockdown.description: Kernel lockdown mode. `Inherit` uses the initramfs kernel's default.

LockdownMode.description: Kernel lockdown mode (see `Documentation/admin-guide/LSM/LoadPin.rst` and the `lockdown` LSM).

oneOf.description: Use the initramfs kernel's built-in default.
Confidentiality mode — integrity + blocks kernel-memory reads.
MS-enrolled VARs — the most common distro-shipped state.
Custom PK/KEK/db from hwsim's test keyring. Tests the custom-CA enrollment path. Keys MUST carry `TEST_ONLY_NOT_FOR_PRODUCTION` in the CN per aegis-boot#226 security constraint #4.
Setup mode — no PK enrolled. Operator MOK enrollment flow.
SB off. Tests the "aegis-boot refuses to flash" diagnostic path.

Quirk.description: A real-world quirk the harness can't simulate. Informational — exposed to scenarios so coverage reports can annotate "this would work here but not on real hardware because X".

description.description: Long-form description of the quirk and its operator impact.

oneOf.description: Scenario skipped for this persona. Should be rare — prefer fixing the scenario over blanket-skipping it.

SecureBoot.description: Secure Boot posture the persona boots under.

custom_keyring.description: When `ovmf_variant == CustomPk`, path to the hwsim-generated test keyring. Must be under `$AEGIS_HWSIM_ROOT/firmware/` — validated at load time per aegis-boot#226 security-engineer constraint #2.

ovmf_variant.description: Which OVMF variant to boot under.

Source.description: Provenance citation for a persona. Every persona must cite its origin so reviewers can trace fields back to a primary source. Matches the `aegis-boot compat` DB's "verified outcomes only" stance.

captured_at.description: ISO-8601 date the persona's fields were captured/verified.

ref_.description: Free-form reference. URL, issue number, spec-sheet title — whatever a reviewer can use to verify the persona's claims.

SourceKind.description: Kind of provenance citation. Ordered from highest to lowest confidence.

oneOf.description: Closed `hardware-report` GitHub issue from a real operator who ran the full flash → boot → kexec chain. Highest confidence.
fwupd / LVFS firmware archive URL. Verified against vendor metadata.
Vendor-published spec sheet (Lenovo PSREF, Dell Product Support, Framework Marketplace). Lowest-confidence; use only for fields the other two don't cover.

Tpm.description: TPM emulation config. `None` variant means no TPM at all.

firmware_version.description: TPM2 firmware version string. Optional. Use when testing firmware-version-gated behavior.

manufacturer.description: TPM2 manufacturer code (IFX, NTC, AMD, STM, INTC, ...). Optional; most tests don't care. Set when validating TPM2-FW-bug-specific paths.

version.description: TPM version, or `"none"` for no TPM. swtpm emulates 1.2 and 2.0.
